hypothesis: If a score-based diffusion model is trained only on metastable-state molecular dynamics data with classifier-free guidance and used to generate candidates via score-based interpolation (isodensity or averaging) during reverse SDE sampling followed by Score-Aligned Ascent refinement using r_SAA = s^B - s^A as an approximate reaction coordinate, then ASTRA will produce accurate transition state configurations (committor ≈ 0.5) and realistic transition pathways across diverse systems (2D potentials, alanine dipeptide, chignolin, and a chemical reaction) with higher success rates and fidelity than linear/geodesic interpolation baselines and ablated SBI or SAA variants.
